rag_rules: Validator diagnostics must be resolved by repairing only the specific AGREE contract elements that cause errors, without modifying unrelated architecture.
AGREE contracts must be placed in valid AADL component type or implementation scopes as per annex location rules.
generic_rules: AGREE contracts should reference only visible identifiers and declared helpers from the same annex scope.
When AGREE reports type mismatches, align helper declarations with their expressions' actual types (int, real, bool).
Generated AGREE code must not introduce package-qualified identifiers or types not present in the model's configured dependencies.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE contracts are enclosed in `annex agree {** ... **};` blocks within component type or implementation annexes.
Helper declarations like `eq` and `const` must use valid scalar types: int, real, bool.
anti_patterns: Avoid introducing new package references or data types in AGREE contracts that are not declared in the AADL model.
Do not attempt to fix AGREE errors by adding new architecture elements outside the scope of the diagnostic.
irrelevant_topics: AADL Inspector diagnostics about missing packages or property sets
AGREE validator diagnostics (none present in this case)
